stdout:
## Success criteria from issue description - ✅ Montgomery internals and API preservation are implemented: `Fp

` stores Montgomery form and converts at API boundaries (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs:35,105-113,126-131,173-190`; `crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/montgomery.rs:74-103`). - ⚠️ Cross-verification is strong but not exact to the stated criterion: exhaustive checks for `Fp<3>` exist (`mod.rs:629-639`), and 2000-case proptests cover `Fp<65537>` and `Fp<2^61-1>` (`mod.rs:690-758`), but there is no explicit **10,000+ random-ops harness including `Fp<3>`**. - ❌ Benchmark acceptance is not evidenced/enforced: naive `%` baselines exist (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs:117-201`) but there is no in-repo threshold check/report proving `>=1.5x` mul and `>=2x` add/sub/inv speedups. ## Separation of concerns - ✅ Changes are confined to `gf2-core` (`src/gfp/*`, `benches/fp_montgomery.rs`), and there is no `gf2-core -> gf2-coding` dependency introduced. ## Functional paradigm and performance - ✅ High-level API remains clean and trait-based; low-level Montgomery code uses efficient bit/word arithmetic. - ✅ Hot arithmetic paths are branchless in Montgomery helpers (`montgomery.rs:74-83,111-127`), aligning with constant-time intent. ## Correctness - ✅ REDC constants and conversions are validated (`montgomery.rs:163-270`), and arithmetic/inversion behavior is broadly cross-checked (`mod.rs:629-760`, `montgomery.rs:276-425`). - ✅ BitVec tail-masking/bit-numbering invariants are unaffected (no touched `bitvec` mutation paths). ## Testing - ✅ Substantial unit/exhaustive/property coverage exists for the new arithmetic (`mod.rs` and `montgomery.rs` tests). - ✅ Current gate artifacts show green fmt/clippy/tests on this commit (`.jit/gate-runs/700bc089...: status passed`, `9151b333...: status passed`, `51e46ef0...: status passed`). - ❌ Success-criterion-specific benchmark proof is missing, so test/validation acceptance is incomplete for this issue. ## Unsafe isolation - ✅ No new `unsafe` in touched `gf2-core` files; unsafe remains isolated outside this area. ## Documentation - ✅ Public `Fp

` docs and examples were updated for Montgomery semantics (`mod.rs:1-31,42-71,90-132`). - ⚠️ No benchmark-result documentation or threshold evidence was added, leaving acceptance evidence debt. ## Prior review feedback for this issue - ✅ Naive add/sub baselines were added since earlier feedback (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs:139-169`). - ❌ The key unresolved item remains: required speedup thresholds are still not demonstrated/enforced in-repo. ## Jit issue dependencies - ✅ Prerequisite `350bff7f` is marked `done` and this implementation builds on that baseline. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
